description: data/make_data.py nulls out 30% of customer_id values (60/200 rows) instead of the original 4% (8/200). transform() calls df.dropna(subset=['customer_id']) with no logging or threshold check, so a third of all orders are silently discarded before aggregation, badly undercounting revenue and order counts per customer/month.
expected_failure: tests/test_pipeline.py::test_known_aggregate fails: pinned cust_0 Dec-2025 aggregate (3, 423.10) no longer matches actual (count/total drop because 2 of those 3 orders had customer_id nulled and were dropped)
fix_hint: The 30% null rate is implausibly high for this pipeline's assumptions; restore data/make_data.py's null injection to a realistic rate (or add explicit handling/alerting in transform() for high null-drop fractions) so aggregation isn't silently corrupted.
